title: 9 Herbal Creams For Asthma
intro_desc: Herbal creams can help relieve asthma symptoms by reducing inflammation and opening airways. Some herbs, like Eucalyptus globulus, have natural decongestant properties that help loosen mucus and make breathing easier. When applied topically as a cream, it can provide quick relief from wheezing and coughing.  Another herb, Rosmarinus officinalis, or rosemary, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe. It can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can trigger asthma attacks.  Echinacea purpurea is an herb that's often used to boost the immune system, which can help prevent asthma attacks by keeping the body healthy. When applied as a cream, it can help soothe and calm the skin and airways, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.  Using herbal creams for asthma can bring many benefits, including reduced reliance on medications, fewer side effects, and improved quality of life. By applying these creams topically, you can quickly and safely relieve asthma symptoms without the need for inhalers or other medications. This can be especially helpful for people who experience frequent or severe asthma attacks, or those who are sensitive to certain medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains eucalyptol, a compound that helps ease congestion and open airways. The creams also contain cineole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the airways and make breathing easier. Eucalyptol and cineole help to thin mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear from the lungs. This can help people with asthma breathe more easily and manage their symptoms. The anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties of eucalyptol and cineole in Eucalyptus globulus creams can help make breathing more comfortable.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for asthma
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 1 tablespoon of eucalyptus essential oil, and a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler over low heat, stirring until smooth.
Remove the double boiler from heat and stir in the eucalyptus essential oil.
Slowly add the distilled water to the mixture while stirring constantly until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify before use as an asthma relief cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains carnosic acid, carnosol, and rosmarinic acid as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma. Rosmarinic acid also has antioxidant properties that protect the body from oxidative stress, which can trigger asthma attacks. The anti-inflammatory properties of carnosic acid and carnosol also help relax the muscles in the airways, allowing for easier airflow. By reducing inflammation and relaxing airway muscles, Rosmarinus officinalis creams may help alleviate asthma symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for asthma
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of rosemary essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add the rosemary essential oil and peppermint essential oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify completely before using it to help relieve asthma symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains a combination of active constituents, including alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce airway inflammation and alleviate asthma symptoms. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea creams have been shown to inhibit the release of chemical mediators that contribute to inflammation, making it a useful remedy for asthma. The glycosides in the cream also have antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the airways. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Echinacea purpurea creams may help to alleviate asthma symptoms and improve lung function.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for asthma
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of coconut oil in a clean bowl.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean bowl, pressing on the solids to get all the liquid.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with the liquid and heat it gently until the beeswax is melted, then pour the mixture into small containers to cool and set.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains a compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the airways. It also contains a compound called shogaol, which has bronchodilatory properties that help widen airways and make breathing easier. The anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties of Zingiber officinale creams can help alleviate symptoms of asthma, such as wheezing and shortness of breath. Gingerol and shogaol also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and widening airways, Zingiber officinale creams can provide relief for people with asthma.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for asthma
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale oil (ginger oil), and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second intervals, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until combined.
Stir in ginger oil and vitamin E oil. Pour the mixture into a bowl and let it cool until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency.
Transfer the Zingiber officinale cream to an airtight container and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks. Apply a small amount to the skin as needed to help alleviate asthma symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a compound called curcumin, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways and prevent oxidative stress. It also contains another compound called bisdemethoxycurcumin, which has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, further reducing inflammation. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of curcuma longa creams help to open up the airways, making it easier to breathe, and reduce the frequency and severity of asthma attacks. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, curcuma longa creams provide relief from asthma symptoms and improve lung function.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for asthma
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder, and 10 drops of lavender essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the melted mixture from the heat and stir in the turmeric powder until well combined.
Add the lavender essential oil and stir well. Let the mixture cool slightly until it reaches 120°F to 130°F.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once set, your Curcuma longa cream for asthma is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains linalool and linalyl acetate, two active compounds that help ease breathing difficulties associated with asthma. These volatile oils have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in airways and improve lung function. They also have a bronchodilating effect, allowing air to flow more freely and making it easier to breathe. Additionally, linalool has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety that can trigger asthma attacks. By reducing inflammation and opening up airways, Lavandula angustifolia creams may help alleviate asthma symptoms and improve quality of life.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for asthma
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Combine dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat the mixture on low heat for 30 minutes, then strain it and discard the flowers.
In a separate saucepan, melt shea butter and beeswax over low heat. Stir until smooth and combined.
Combine the strained Lavandula angustifolia oil with the shea butter and beeswax mixture. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and add vitamin E oil. Let it cool and solidify before using it as a cream for asthma relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids, bilobalide, and ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce airway inflammation associated with asthma. These compounds also improve blood flow to the lungs, allowing for easier breathing. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have a natural bronchodilating effect, helping to open up the airways and improve airflow. Ginkgolides block platelet-activating factor (PAF), a substance that causes inflammation in the airways, further reducing asthma symptoms. By reducing inflammation and improving lung function, Ginkgo biloba creams can help to alleviate asthma symptoms and improve quality of life.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for asthma
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba extract, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add the Ginkgo biloba extract and shea butter to the melted mixture. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a small container or tin. Let it cool and harden completely.
Once hardened, the Ginkgo biloba cream is ready to use. Apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day, or as needed, to help relieve asthma symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains thujone, rosmarinic acid, and borneol as its bioactive constituents. These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate asthma symptoms. Thujone has a bronchodilating effect, which means it widens the airways and makes it easier to breathe. Rosmarinic acid reduces inflammation in the lungs, while borneol helps relax the airway muscles, making it simpler to inhale and exhale. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the airway muscles, Salvia officinalis creams can provide relief from asthma symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for asthma
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves. You can buy them at a health food store or online.
Combine the leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar. Discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil to the liquid mixture. Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler until the beeswax is melted and the mixture is smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol and carvacrol, two bioactive constituents that help with asthma symptoms. Thymol is a natural expectorant, which means it helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Carvacrol has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and calm the airway muscles, allowing for more free-flowing air. The antimicrobial properties of thymol also prevent the growth of bacteria and viruses that can trigger asthma attacks. By reducing inflammation, loosening mucus, and preventing infections, Thymus vulgaris creams can help alleviate asthma symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for asthma
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves and 2 cups of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Thymus vulgaris leaves to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter into the oil and let it cool until solidified, then whip into a creamy texture.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent asthma from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and eucalyptus, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the airways. Ginger tea is also thought to have a similar effect. These teas may help reduce the risk of asthma by keeping airways open and relaxed.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for asthma can be a good option, but it's essential to consume them in moderation. Some teas, like eucalyptus and peppermint, can help ease breathing and open airways. However, drinking too much can cause an imbalance in your body's natural chemistry, which might worsen asthma symptoms.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas may help with asthma symptoms, but it's hard to predict exactly when you'll see results. Some people notice a difference within a few days to a week after drinking the tea regularly. It might take a bit longer, like two to four weeks, for your symptoms to really start to calm down.
faq_time: For asthma, it's often best to drink herbal tea in the morning. This can help clear airways and start the day feeling more relaxed. As you're already awake, it's a good time to take a moment to breathe and settle in.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for asthma. For example, ginkgo biloba can make asthma symptoms worse. Peppermint tea may lower blood pressure, which could be a problem if you're taking medication to control blood pressure and have asthma. Some herbal teas can slow down or speed up the liver's processing of medications.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's essential to be cautious with herbal teas, especially if you have asthma. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are usually safe. However, teas containing ephedra or licorice root might not be suitable as they can cause problems. Always check the ingredients and choose teas that are specifically labeled as safe for pregnant women.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for asthma can be a good option, but if you're breastfeeding, it's best to be cautious. Some herbal teas, like ephedra and licorice root, contain compounds that might pass into breast milk and affect the baby. However, others like peppermint and ginger are generally considered safe in moderation.
title_2: 9 Natural Asthma Creams for Smokers' Loved Ones
